description: The outside limit of an object, area, or surface; the line where something ends. 'Shuna' names the meeting point—where one thing stops and another begins, the line of transition.
sound_symbolism: The word marks the limit. 'Shu' /ʃu/ begins with soft precision, boundary sensed. Then 'na' settles at the line, edge defined.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo shuna' (the edge). Related to 'thorui' (boundary) but emphasizes the line itself rather than the concept of limit.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Edges are where awareness lives—the boundary between self and other, inner and outer.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Edges defined form—the flowing lines that separated figure from ground, creating shape through boundary.
